insights.claim: ZIPs in the most-deprived tenth (by ADI) average 4.2 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted decile means over Area Deprivation Index.

insights.claim: Across ZIPs, copd is most associated with median household income (Spearman ρ=-0.644, n=29,492).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation, ecological (ZIP-level); not causal.
Maximum ZIP value (ties broken by population).

insights.claim: About 11.4 million people live in ZIPs above the high-burden threshold (11.5%).
insights.method_note: Sum of population in ZIPs at or above the 90th percentile (11.5%).
